architecture.summary: An image captioning system that extends Semantic Compositional Networks (SCN) with Tensor Product Representation (TPR) attention mechanisms. Visual features (ResNet 2048-D + Tag features 999-D) and semantic tags are encoded; TPR decomposes word embeddings and hidden states using orthogonal Hadamard matrices to create structured, compositionally interpretable feature spaces. These decomposed representations (Embedding, Hidden, and/or decomposed TPR variants) are fused and fed into an SCN-LSTM decoder that generates captions word-by-word. Optionally, Self-Critical Sequence Training (SCST) reinforcement learning refines weights using CIDEr-D rewards.
trained_components: TPR role vectors and unbinding operations
embedding and hidden state decomposition MLPs

biblio.title: Feature Fusion Effects of Tensor Product Representation on (De)Compositional Network for Caption Generation for Images

claims.statement: TPR enables better and unique representation and structuring of the feature space and will enable better sentence composition from these representations
Embedding + dTPR architecture achieves BLEU_4 of 0.338
Hidden + Embedding + TPR + RL achieves BLEU_4 of 0.338 with Self-Critical Sequence Training reinforcement learning
Retrieval rate of TPR unbinding (Equation 9) is 100% correct for word embeddings like Word2Vec and GloVe
Decomposition of Embedding and Hidden components with TPR attention unsaturates LSTM memory and prevents appearance of similar word sequences, enabling better caption generation
Embedding + dTPR performance may degrade when applied with SCST reinforcement learning due to overfitting
Hidden + Embedding + dTPR architecture achieved lower performance than Embedding + dTPR and Hidden + dTPR configurations, suggesting over-decomposition reduces effectiveness
Tag features combined with image features (tag + img fusion) achieves maximum performance on MSCOCO with correlation-based fusion being a turning point for image captioning
